# Transaction Services Profitability Normalization

Session ID: `v1-finance-archetype-20260206-185405`
Updated: `2026-02-06T18:54:12+00:00`

## 1. P&L Walkthrough
- Section ID: `p-l-walkthrough`
- Intent: `analysis`
- Layout: `table-insights-two-column`
- Variant: `white`
- Status: `draft`

| Line item | FY22 | FY23 | TTM Jun-24 |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Revenue | 512 | 588 | 624 |
| Gross profit | 392 | 459 | 491 |
| EBITDA | 118 | 147 | 161 |
| EBITDA margin | 23.0% | 25.0% | 25.8% |
- Revenue growth is mainly transaction volume-led with selective pricing uplift in enterprise channels.
- Gross margin expansion reflects better pass-through recovery and processing efficiency.
- EBITDA conversion improved despite reinvestment in support capacity.

## 2. Margin Bridge
- Section ID: `margin-bridge`
- Intent: `multi-driver-analysis`
- Layout: `2-column-chart`
- Variant: `white`
- Status: `draft`

- Baseline margin FY22: 23.0
- Mix shift contribution: 1.2
- Pricing contribution: 0.9
- Productivity contribution: 1.1
- Inflation and support drag: -0.4
- Net margin TTM Jun-24: 25.8
- Bridge indicates expansion is quality-led rather than one-off accounting effects.

## 3. QofE-Style Adjustments
- Section ID: `qofe-style-adjustments`
- Intent: `recommendations`
- Layout: `recommendations-white`
- Variant: `white`
- Status: `draft`

- Adj #1: remove non-recurring settlement credit (+2.1 EBITDA impact).
- Adj #2: normalize staffing to run-rate after temporary vacancy lag (-1.3 EBITDA impact).
- Adj #3: align support software costs to contracted run-rate (-0.7 EBITDA impact).
- Reported EBITDA margin: 25.8%; normalized EBITDA margin: 25.1%.
- Interpretation: earnings quality remains strong; normalization reduces upside narrative but improves comparability.

## 4. Net Working Capital Walkthrough
- Section ID: `net-working-capital-walkthrough`
- Intent: `risk`
- Layout: `risks-mitigations`
- Variant: `white`
- Status: `draft`

- Risk: DSO expansion in Q2 inflates reported working capital need.
- Risk: AP timing normalization may reduce temporary cash benefit in H2.
- Risk: settlement receivable volatility can distort month-end liquidity view.
- Mitigation: implement weekly receivables intervention for top 20 aging accounts.
- Mitigation: align payment scheduling policy with vendor criticality tiers.
- Mitigation: add liquidity trigger thresholds tied to DSO and conversion rates.
